controller.description: Controller behaviour (maps onto the binary's flags).

allowedActionHosts.description:
Host globs permitted for http actions (SSRF guard). Loopback/link-local are
always denied unless explicitly listed here.
allowedActionHosts.title: allowedActionHosts

defaultInterval.description:
Reconcile cadence for StageSets that omit spec.interval. Most StageSets can
leave spec.interval unset and inherit this.
defaultInterval.title: defaultInterval

inventoryMode.description: Inventory strategy for tracking applied objects.
inventoryMode.title: inventoryMode

leaderElect.description: Leader election for HA replicas. Keep on; harmless single-replica.
leaderElect.title: leaderElect

runbookBaseURL.description:
URL prefix appended to actionable Ready messages as (runbook: <base>/<reason>/).
Defaults to the documentation site; override to a fork or an internal mirror,
or set empty to disable the runbook links.
runbookBaseURL.title: runbookBaseURL

watchNamespaces.description:
Namespaces this controller watches. Empty (the default) means cluster-wide.
When set, the manager's cache only observes StageSets and sources in these
namespaces, and RBAC pivots from a cluster-wide ClusterRoleBinding to one
RoleBinding per listed namespace — the multi-tenant controller-instances
pattern (one release per tenant-group, disjoint watch sets).
watchNamespaces.title: watchNamespaces

controller.title: controller

crds.description:
CRD lifecycle. The chart ships the controller's CRDs under templates/ so a
`helm upgrade` applies schema changes automatically. Set create=false to
manage them out-of-band — e.g. pre-installed cluster-wide, or in CI where the
same chart is installed once per values file and a kept cluster-scoped CRD
owned by the first release can't be re-adopted by the next.
crds.title: crds

deployment.description: Extra labels and revision history for the Deployment.
deployment.title: deployment

gate.description:
Flagger stage-gate endpoint (GET /gate/{ns}/{stageset}/{stage}). Renders a
dedicated Service when enabled.
gate.title: gate

global.description: Global values are values that can be accessed from any chart or subchart by exactly the same name.
global.title: global

image.description:
Container image. tag defaults to .Chart.AppVersion (the released controller
version); override only for a custom build.

pullPolicy.description:
Pull policy. IfNotPresent suits tagged release images; switch to Always for
a mutable tag (latest, edge).
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

tag.description: Explicit tag override; empty defaults to .Chart.AppVersion.
tag.title: tag

image.title: image

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for a private registry.
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

prometheusRule.description:
Opt-in PrometheusRule with a starter alert set on the custom stageset_* metrics
plus controller-runtime signals. Requires the Prometheus operator CRDs.

annotations.description: Extra annotations on the PrometheusRule object.
annotations.title: annotations

extraAlertLabels.description:
Labels merged onto EVERY rendered alert — route all stageset alerts through
one Alertmanager receiver (e.g. team: platform).
extraAlertLabels.title: extraAlertLabels

extraRules.description:
Extra alert rules appended under a separate "stageset-extras" group, rendered
verbatim. To silence a built-in alert, raise its threshold below to an
impossibly high value rather than forking the chart.
extraRules.title: extraRules

interval.description: Evaluation interval for every rule group.
interval.title: interval

labels.description:
Extra labels on the PrometheusRule object (typically what your Prometheus
selects on).
labels.title: labels

runbookAnnotationKey.description:
Annotation key the runbook URL lands under (runbook_url is the
Prometheus-operator convention).
runbookAnnotationKey.title: runbookAnnotationKey

runbookBaseURL.description:
Prefix for per-alert runbook page links. Defaults to the documentation site;
the per-reason alert appends /<reason>/, fixed alerts append /<page>/.
runbookBaseURL.title: runbookBaseURL

thresholds.description:
Numeric/duration thresholds in one place so operators can tune the noise
floor without editing rule bodies.

podDownDuration.description:
StageSetControllerPodDown: NotReady duration before paging. Below 5m it
fires during normal pod rolls.
podDownDuration.title: podDownDuration

reconcileErrorRate.description:
StageSetReconcileErrorsHigh: per-StageSet Ready=False rate (per second,
5m window). 0.1/s ≈ one failure every 10s — a stuck StageSet, not a blip.
reconcileErrorRate.title: reconcileErrorRate

reconcileLatencySeconds.description:
StageSetReconcileLatencyHigh: reconcile p99 ceiling, seconds. 30s is
generous — an artifact fetch plus a multi-stage apply.
reconcileLatencySeconds.title: reconcileLatencySeconds

webhookCertRenewalFailuresPerHour.description:
StageSetWebhookCertRenewalFailing: self-signed cert renewal failures per
hour before paging.
webhookCertRenewalFailuresPerHour.title: webhookCertRenewalFailuresPerHour

workqueueDepth.description:
StageSetControllerWorkqueueDepthHigh: queued reconcile requests before
alerting. 50 absorbs a normal burst, low enough to catch a real stall.
workqueueDepth.title: workqueueDepth

thresholds.title: thresholds

prometheusRule.title: prometheusRule

namespace.description:
Install namespace with Pod-Security labels. The chart installs into
.Release.Namespace; set create to render it with PSS labels.
namespace.title: namespace

networkPolicy.description: Opt-in NetworkPolicy. Inert without a NetworkPolicy controller.

additionalIngress.description: Extra ingress rules merged verbatim. Applies to the kubernetes engine only.
additionalIngress.title: additionalIngress

calico.description:
Calico engine (engine=calico) raw passthrough. Entries are merged verbatim
into the projectcalico.org NetworkPolicy's spec.ingress / spec.egress.

egress.description: Extra spec.egress rules merged verbatim (Calico NetworkPolicy schema).
egress.title: egress

ingress.description: Extra spec.ingress rules merged verbatim (Calico NetworkPolicy schema).
ingress.title: ingress

calico.title: calico

cilium.description:
Cilium engine (engine=cilium) raw passthrough. Entries are merged verbatim
into the CiliumNetworkPolicy's spec.ingress / spec.egress. This is how you
tighten the cilium policy — e.g. add fromEndpoints to the ingress rules, or
additional toEndpoints / toFQDNs egress.

egress.description: Extra spec.egress rules merged verbatim (CiliumNetworkPolicy schema).
egress.title: egress

ingress.description: Extra spec.ingress rules merged verbatim (CiliumNetworkPolicy schema).
ingress.title: ingress

cilium.title: cilium

clusterNetworkPolicy.description:
ClusterNetworkPolicy engine (engine=clusterNetworkPolicy) raw passthrough.
Entries are merged verbatim into the policy.networking.k8s.io
ClusterNetworkPolicy's spec.ingress / spec.egress.

egress.description: Extra spec.egress rules merged verbatim (ClusterNetworkPolicy schema).
egress.title: egress

ingress.description: Extra spec.ingress rules merged verbatim (ClusterNetworkPolicy schema).
ingress.title: ingress

priority.description: spec.priority on the ClusterNetworkPolicy (lower wins within the tier).
priority.title: priority

clusterNetworkPolicy.title: clusterNetworkPolicy

defaultDeny.description:
Render a namespace-wide default-deny policy so EVERY pod in the namespace
is denied by default and the per-workload allowlists are the only exceptions
(zero-trust namespace). OFF by default because it also denies co-located
workloads — only enable it when the controller has its own namespace. Leaving
it off keeps a pod-scoped setup: only this controller's pods are locked down,
neighbours untouched.

order.description:
Calico/ClusterNetworkPolicy sort key for the deny-all. It must rank the
deny-all AFTER the per-workload allowlists so the allowlists win: Calico
evaluates lower order first (the allowlist policies carry no order = lowest
precedence, so this defaults high), and ClusterNetworkPolicy gives lower
priority numbers higher precedence (the allowlist priority defaults to
networkPolicy.clusterNetworkPolicy.priority, so this defaults higher). The
kubernetes and cilium engines have no precedence knob — deny + allow simply
combine additively, allow wins.
order.title: order

defaultDeny.title: defaultDeny

dns.description:
When egress is enabled, allow DNS (UDP+TCP 53) to the cluster DNS
namespace.
dns.title: dns

dnsNamespace.description:
Namespace of the cluster DNS service (matched by
kubernetes.io/metadata.name).
dnsNamespace.title: dnsNamespace

enabled.description:
Render egress rules. Adds Egress to policyTypes, so everything not
explicitly allowed is denied. Off by default: enable only after allowing
everything the controller needs (DNS, the kube-apiserver,
source-controller / producer namespaces, S3, the OTLP collector, and any
remote-cluster or http-action targets) via egress.to — enabling egress
without allowing the apiserver cuts the controller off from the cluster.
enabled.title: enabled

to.description:
Additional egress peers merged verbatim into spec.egress. This is where
you allow the kube-apiserver (an ipBlock CIDR), the source-controller /
producer namespaces, and external endpoints (S3, OTLP, remote-cluster or
http-action targets). NetworkPolicy cannot select the apiserver by label,
so it must be an ipBlock here.
to.title: to

enabled.description:
Render the NetworkPolicy. When enabled, the policy targets the controller
pod; with a NetworkPolicy controller (Calico/Cilium/etc.) installed,
anything not listed below is denied. Leave OFF on clusters without such a
controller — the policy is silently inert there.
enabled.title: enabled

engine.description:
Policy engine to render for. kubernetes (default) emits a vanilla
networking.k8s.io NetworkPolicy. cilium / calico / clusterNetworkPolicy emit
the engine-native equivalent instead (CiliumNetworkPolicy,
projectcalico.org NetworkPolicy, or policy.networking.k8s.io
ClusterNetworkPolicy). The per-port .from knobs below apply to the kubernetes
engine only; alternative engines are pod-scoped allow-all on the required
ports and tighten via their native passthrough lists
(cilium/calico/clusterNetworkPolicy .ingress / .egress).
engine.title: engine

gate.description:
Sources allowed to reach the gate port. Empty = all (e.g. Flagger for the
gate).
gate.title: gate

metrics.description:
Sources allowed to reach the metrics port. Empty = all (e.g. Prometheus
for metrics).
metrics.title: metrics

webhook.description:
Sources allowed to reach the webhook port. Empty = all (e.g.
kube-apiserver for the webhook, which cannot be a podSelector).
Applies to the kubernetes engine only.
webhook.title: webhook

networkPolicy.title: networkPolicy

pod.description: Extra labels for the pod template.
pod.title: pod

rbac.description:
Controller RBAC. The default grant is deliberately minimal: the controller
impersonates each StageSet's spec.serviceAccountName for cluster writes, so a
tenant's own RBAC bounds what its StageSets can touch.

clusterAdmin.description:
Bind the controller ServiceAccount to the built-in cluster-admin ClusterRole.
Single-tenant clusters where StageSets omit spec.serviceAccountName run the
controller under its own identity; cluster-admin lets it apply any kind
cluster-wide — the model Flux's helm-controller uses in its default install.
Leave false for multi-tenant clusters, where every StageSet sets
serviceAccountName and the controller only impersonates those scoped SAs.
clusterAdmin.title: clusterAdmin

rbac.title: rbac

replicas.description:
Replica bounds. min is the Deployment replica count; when max > min an HPA and
a PodDisruptionBudget are rendered. At the default 1/1 both are omitted.
replicas.title: replicas

resources.description:
Pod resource sizing. Applied to both requests and limits (cpu, memory, and
ephemeral-storage) so the pod is fully constrained — kube-score's
resource-completeness checks require all three on both sides.
resources.title: resources

rollbackStore.description: Optional rollback store for bit-exact, GC-independent rollbacks.

backend.description:
none: rollback falls back to re-fetching the producer artifact. pvc: an RWX
PersistentVolumeClaim (use RWX for HA replicas). s3: any S3-compatible bucket.
backend.title: backend

existingSecret.description:
Secret holding accessKey / secretKey / sessionToken keys. Empty static
creds engage minio-go's IAM/IRSA discovery chain.
existingSecret.title: existingSecret

sse.description:
Server-side encryption at rest for stored objects. The rollback store holds
rendered Secret data, so this defaults on. s3 = SSE-S3 (bucket-managed key);
kms = SSE-KMS with sseKmsKeyId; none only for a backend that cannot honor an
SSE header. A rejected SSE write is non-fatal — it warns and skips the store
write, the rollout still succeeds.
sse.title: sse

sseKmsKeyId.description: KMS key ARN/ID for sse: kms; empty uses the bucket's default KMS key.
sseKmsKeyId.title: sseKmsKeyId

rollbackStore.title: rollbackStore

securityContext.description:
Container-level securityContext (PSS restricted). runAsUser/runAsGroup are
set above 10000 (kube-score requirement) for a static binary that needs no
real user identity.
securityContext.title: securityContext

annotations.description:
Extra annotations on the ServiceAccount — e.g. eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn for
IRSA, so the controller's S3 rollback store assumes an IAM role instead of
using static keys.
annotations.title: annotations

serviceMesh.description:
Opt-in service-mesh L7 authorization + mTLS for the controller pod.
Complementary to networkPolicy, NOT a replacement: networkPolicy is L3/L4
(which pods/IPs may open a connection), serviceMesh is L7/identity (which mesh
identities may call which port). Both can be enabled at once and stack
additively. Authorizes only the mesh-reachable ports — gate (Flagger) and
metrics (Prometheus). The webhook (kube-apiserver) and health/probe (kubelet)
ports are deliberately left out: those callers speak plain TLS / no mesh
identity, and locking them to mesh principals would break admission and health
probing.

defaultDeny.description:
Render a namespace-wide default-deny so every pod in the install namespace
rejects unauthorized mesh traffic and the per-workload allows above are the
only exceptions (zero-trust namespace). Istio renders an empty-spec
AuthorizationPolicy (deny-all) scoped to the whole namespace, which sits at
lower precedence than the workload ALLOW. Linkerd has no per-object deny-all;
the namespace default is set via the config.linkerd.io/default-inbound-policy
annotation, so this is stamped onto the chart-managed Namespace (requires
namespace.create=true) — otherwise annotate the namespace out-of-band. Enable
only when the controller owns its namespace; it also denies co-located
workloads.
defaultDeny.title: defaultDeny

enabled.description:
Opt-in. When enabled, the chart renders the selected engine's
authorization + mTLS objects for the controller pod. Inert unless that mesh
(Istio or Linkerd) is actually installed and the pod is injected.
enabled.title: enabled

engine.description:
Mesh engine to render for. istio emits security.istio.io
AuthorizationPolicy (+ optional PeerAuthentication). linkerd emits
policy.linkerd.io Server / AuthorizationPolicy / MeshTLSAuthentication
objects.
engine.title: engine

gate.description:
Per-port allowed mesh identities. Each `from` entry is a source matcher:
  - principals: SPIFFE/mesh identities (Istio source.principals, e.g.
      cluster.local/ns/<ns>/sa/<sa>). For Linkerd these map to
      MeshTLSAuthentication identities (the proxy identity string, e.g.
      <sa>.<ns>.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local, or "*").
  - namespaces: source namespaces (Istio source.namespaces). ISTIO-ONLY —
      Linkerd authenticates by workload identity, not by namespace, so this
      field is ignored on the linkerd engine.
An EMPTY `from` list on a port means OPEN (allow any caller), mirroring
networkPolicy's empty-`from`=open semantics. On Istio that is a port rule
with no `from`; on Linkerd it is a MeshTLSAuthentication of identities: ["*"]
(any authenticated meshed client).
Mesh identities allowed to poll the Flagger stage-gate endpoint
(ports.gate). Rendered only when gate.enabled. Empty = open.
gate.title: gate

istio.description:
Istio engine raw passthrough. Entries are merged verbatim into the
AuthorizationPolicy's spec.rules (security.istio.io/v1 rule schema). Use this
to add rules the per-port `from` knobs above can't express — path/method
matchers, `when` JWT-claim conditions, ipBlocks, etc.
istio.title: istio

linkerd.description:
Linkerd engine raw passthrough. Entries are appended verbatim as
additional documents after the rendered Server / AuthorizationPolicy /
MeshTLSAuthentication set — each entry must be a complete object
(policy.linkerd.io AuthorizationPolicy, HTTPRoute, etc.).
linkerd.title: linkerd

metrics.description:
Mesh identities allowed to scrape the controller metrics port
(ports.metrics). Empty = open.
metrics.title: metrics

mtls.description:
mTLS posture (Istio engine only). Empty defers to the mesh's own default
(mesh-wide PeerAuthentication / MeshConfig). permissive renders a
PeerAuthentication accepting both mTLS and plaintext. strict requires mTLS on
the workload's ports EXCEPT the webhook + health ports, which get a
port-level PERMISSIVE carve-out so the non-mesh kube-apiserver and kubelet
still connect. Linkerd negotiates mTLS automatically between meshed pods, so
this knob does not apply to the linkerd engine.
mtls.title: mtls

serviceMesh.title: serviceMesh

certManager.description: cert-manager mode: the issuer that signs the webhook Certificate.
certManager.title: certManager

certMode.description:
TLS provisioning. self-signed (default) has the controller generate a CA +
serving cert in-pod and patch the ValidatingWebhookConfiguration caBundle
(HA-safe) — no cluster prerequisite, works out of the box. cert-manager
renders a Certificate (cert mounted from the issued Secret) and needs
cert-manager + a configured issuer.
certMode.title: certMode

certValidity.description: Validity of the self-signed serving cert; the renewer rotates at validity/3.
certValidity.title: certValidity
